intro: Anthericum krauseanum, also known as Star of Bethlehem, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family. Its roots and bulbs have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate stress, anxiety, and insomnia due to their sedative and anxiolytic properties, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Anthericum krauseanum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Anthericum krauseanum are teas, tinctures, and capsules, often used to support nervous system health and as a natural sleep aid.
